title: cathartolinum catharticum
side_effects: Cathartolinum catharticum can have side effects if used improperly, such as severe diarrhea, dehydration, and electrolyte imbalance. Prolonged use of the plant can also lead to intestinal irritation, nausea, and stomach cramps. In rare cases, excessive use of cathartolinum catharticum can cause kidney damage and heart problems due to the sudden loss of fluids and electrolytes. If not managed properly, these side effects can be life-threatening and require immediate medical attention.
